tool.name: ANGSD
tool.description: Analysis of Next Generation Sequencing Data
tool.command:
## 1. Link BAM files with proper .bam extensions touch bam.filelist && #set $i = 0 #for $bam in $input_bams: ln -s '$bam' 'sample_${i}.bam' && #if $bam.metadata.bam_index: ln -s '$bam.metadata.bam_index' 'sample_${i}.bam.bai' && #else: samtools index 'sample_${i}.bam' && #end if echo 'sample_${i}.bam' >> bam.filelist && #set $i = $i + 1 #end for
## 2. Build ANGSD arguments safely using Cheetah variables #set $args = "" #if $ref_genome: #set $args = $args + " -ref '%s'" % $ref_genome #end if #if $gl_model: #set $args = $args + " -GL %s" % $gl_model #end if #if $do_glf: #set $args = $args + " -doGlF %s" % $do_glf #end if #if $do_majorminor: #set $args = $args + " -doMajorMinor %s" % $do_majorminor #end if #if str($do_fasta) != "0": #set $args = $args + " -doFasta %s" % $do_fasta #end if #if str($do_fasta) != "0" or $filters.do_counts: #set $args = $args + " -doCounts 1" #end if #if $filters.i_counts: #set $args = $args + " -iCounts 1" #end if #if str($filters.min_mapq) != "": #set $args = $args + " -minMapQ %s" % $filters.min_mapq #end if #if str($filters.min_q) != "": #set $args = $args + " -minQ %s" % $filters.min_q #end if #if $filters.region: #set $args = $args + " -r '%s'" % $filters.region #end if
## 3. Run ANGSD angsd -bam bam.filelist -out output -nThreads \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1} $args

param.name: input_bams
param.label: Input BAM file(s)
param.help: Select one or more BAM files to analyze.

param.name: do_majorminor
param.label: Estimate Major/Minor Alleles (-doMajorMinor)
param.option: Infer major and minor from GL (1)
Infer major and minor from allele counts (2)

param.name: region
param.label: Region (-r)
param.help: Specify a specific region or chromosome (e.g., 'X:5000000-154900000').

validator.message: Region format must be like 'chr' or 'chr:start-end'

tool.help:
**What it does**
ANGSD is a software for analyzing next generation sequencing data. This wrapper allows you to perform Genotype Likelihood estimation and other utility functions commonly used in ancient DNA pipelines.
